title: AI Benefits Knowledge Base — Multi-Document Intelligence Across All 5 Acme Documents
description: The AI Requirements Studio shall maintain a unified knowledge base indexed from all uploaded Acme documents. The AI shall reason across all five documents simultaneously, not just the most recently uploaded. The knowledge base enables cross-document queries that reveal relationships, conflicts, and gaps a single-document analysis would miss.
architectureNote: Hybrid RAG: PDF text → chunk + embed → vector index + metadata filter; same pattern as REQ-IMPACT-001

title: Benefits Knowledge Assistant — Natural Language Queries Across All Documents
description: The AI Requirements Studio shall include a Benefits Knowledge Assistant chat interface that answers natural language questions sourced from the document corpus. Every answer shall cite the specific document, page, and section it was derived from.
exampleQueries: What is the waiting period for full-time employees?
Which plans allow Employee + Spouse coverage?
What happens if an employee loses coverage mid-year?
Are part-time employees eligible for dental coverage?
answerFormat: Answer | Source: [Document Name, Page N, Section X.X] | Confidence: N%

title: Cross-Document Requirement Aggregation — 'Show All Requirements About Eligibility'
description: The AI shall support cross-document queries that aggregate requirements by topic across all documents. A query like 'Find all requirements related to eligibility' shall return matching requirements from every document, ranked by relevance and annotated with their source.
queryExamples: What enrollment deadlines are defined across all documents?

title: AI Answers Include Source Citation with Document, Page, and Section
description: Every AI response from the Benefits Knowledge Assistant shall include a source citation linking to the specific document, page number, and section heading from which the answer was derived. If an answer synthesizes information from multiple sources, all sources are listed.

citationFormat.textSnippet: Full-time employees working 30 or more hours per week are eligible...

title: AI Flags Incomplete or Contradictory Information in Answers
description: When the AI cannot answer a query with high confidence, or when documents provide contradictory information on the queried topic, the AI shall explicitly state this rather than fabricating a confident answer. The AI shall surface the contradiction as a conflict for human review.
incompleteAnswerExample: The Benefits Guide and Eligibility Policy provide different waiting periods for this scenario. This is flagged as CONFLICT-003 for your review.

title: Document Library View — All Uploaded Documents with Extraction Status and Coverage Summary
description: The AI Requirements Studio shall include a Document Library view listing all uploaded documents with: document name, upload date, extraction status, requirements extracted, conflicts detected, and last query timestamp. Documents are the entry point for both extraction and Knowledge Assistant queries.

title: AI Identifies Missing Requirements — Policy References Process That Doesn't Exist
description: The AI shall detect when a document references a process, verification step, or rule that is not defined anywhere in the document corpus. Missing definitions shall be flagged as MISSING_INFORMATION conflicts. This catches gaps a human reviewer may miss when reading documents sequentially.
missingRequirementExample: The Benefits Guide references 'Dependent Verification Procedures' but no verification process is defined in any uploaded document. Flagged as: MISSING-REQ-001.

title: AI Document Summary for Each Uploaded Document
description: The AI shall generate a structured summary for each uploaded document: document type, primary topic areas, requirement count by category, key policy decisions identified, and notable ambiguities. Summary is generated during extraction and displayed in the Document Library.

title: AI Distinguishes Explicit Language from Inferred Requirements
description: The AI shall explicitly indicate whether a requirement is based on explicit language in the document ('Full-time employees working 30+ hours...') or inferred from context ('Given the 30-day waiting period language and the ACA full-time threshold of 30 hours, the following rule is implied...'). Inferred requirements require additional human scrutiny.

extractionTypes.description: Derived from formula or mathematical relationship stated in document

title: Benefits Knowledge Assistant Accessible in AI Interview Context Window
description: During an AI Interview session, the Benefits Knowledge Assistant knowledge base shall be available as a retrieval source. The AI Interviewer can query the document corpus to provide grounded answers and ask targeted clarifying questions.

title: End-to-End Traceability: Source PDF Page → Knowledge Base → Requirement → Configuration
description: The full provenance chain from a PDF page through the knowledge base extraction to an approved requirement to the configuration it affects shall be navigable in the Audit & Traceability workspace. Both forward (document → configuration) and backward (configuration → document) navigation shall be supported.

title: Knowledge Base Supports Incremental Re-Extraction When Documents Are Updated
description: When an updated version of a document is uploaded, the AI shall re-extract only the changed sections (detected via SHA-256 diff) rather than re-processing the entire document. Previously extracted requirements that are unaffected by the change retain their existing review and approval status.

title: Employee-Facing Benefits Assistant — Limited Scope Query on Own Benefits
description: The Employee persona shall have access to a simplified Benefits Assistant that answers questions about their own plan options and enrollment. This assistant queries only the published plan data and the employee's own enrollment record — never the policy documents or other employees' data.

title: AI Conflict Detection Upgraded to 5-Category Classification Including MISSING_INFORMATION
description: The existing AI conflict detection (REQ-CONFLICT-001) is upgraded to support all five conflict classifications: CONTRADICTION, OVERLAP, AMBIGUITY, MISSING_INFORMATION, and DUPLICATE. Each classification has distinct detection logic, severity defaults, and resolution workflow paths.

title: Document Intelligence Pre-Population of AI Interview Context
description: When an AI Interview session is initiated after document extraction, the interview context shall be pre-populated with the high-confidence facts extracted from all documents. The AI Interviewer starts with existing knowledge and focuses the interview on gaps and ambiguities, not re-asking questions already answered by the documents.
prePopulatedContext: Structured facts extracted with ≥0.90 confidence from all documents; open questions list derived from AMBIGUITY and MISSING_INFORMATION conflicts
